title: The Beautifier Was an Auditor
hook: A skill built to make a README prettier turned out to be a drift detector. Across five repos it flagged a deploy doc still pointing at a platform the project left months ago, a command list that no longer matched the actual commands, and capabilities the code had grown that nobody had written down. The visual polish was the smallest thing it did.
for: Anyone about to run a 'polish' or 'cleanup' pass on their docs — and anyone who trusts a cosmetic tool to stay in its lane.
get: Why a beautifier's real payload is its inspect step, five repos' worth of drift it surfaced, and one honest failure — claiming checked when the check was only partial.
do: Run the audit pass first: fix what's wrong before polishing what's already fine — and verify the render where it actually ships, not just locally.
previewAlt: Demo 09 title slide: a README claim beside the code fact that contradicts it
